message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Rapid production of soluble recombinant antigens is important for developing
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  diagnostics, vaccines, and drugs against virus such as severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2). In this research, hard-to-express nucleocapsid, membrane, and envelope proteins were successfully expressed by an Escherichia coli-based cell-free protein synthesis system. The solubility of the proteins was optimized using various amphipathic molecules. Most of the impurities were easily removed by a one-step Ni-NTA affinity chromatography. This study provides an easy and quick alternative for virus’s trans-membrane and nucleotides associated recombinant protein expression, which has potential downstream application for early screening of newly emerging viruses.
                </jats:p>
title: Cell-free synthesis and purification of recombinant nucleocapsid (N), membrane (M), and envelope (E) proteins
